sections.label: USER PROMPT
sections.content:
The code executed 2 actions in the previous sequence.
Executed actions: UP, RIGHT.
You are still on the same level.
Current state: step 74, level 1.
Valid actions right now: UP, DOWN, LEFT, RIGHT, MOUSE.
Only tool: `python`. It receives `current_frame`, `previous_frame`, `history`, `transitions`, `last_transition`, `valid_actions`, `last_action_result`, and `action(actions)`. It also provides `last_animation` (per-action frames of your last `action(...)` call) and `frame_stats` (how much this game has animated so far) -- optional; use them only if you judge the intermediate motion informative rather than incidental.
Only letter-coded board views and lightweight metadata are exposed; raw numeric color IDs are not available.
Keep tool output compact: use `current_frame.segmentation` as the primary view, and `current_frame.ascii` only for a small specific region; never print full boards.
For the most recent change, compare `previous_frame` to `current_frame`, or `last_transition.before_frame` to `last_transition.after_frame`; `history[-1].frame` is the current frame, not the previous one.
Use Python to inspect the evidence, refine that world model from the newest history, and search or score candidate actions or short sequences against the current goal as you currently understand it.
Maintain a compact working world model of what the current level seems to contain, what actions appear to do, what the goal seems to be, what is still uncertain, and what plan currently looks best.
Below you are provided with the current world model from the previous turn. The default behavior is to copy it and add or remove things based on the evidence that you gathered. BEFORE EXECUTING NEW ACTIONS YOU MUST ALWAYS GIVE THE REVISED VERSION OF THE WORLD MODEL.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it, but stop immediately if a result reports `game_over`, `level_completed`, or `done`.
Working world model carried from earlier turns:
- World model: Left green W at (30-32, 24-26) in corridor. Right green c at (30-32, 45-47) in right panel. Right dark outline at (26-30, 44-48). Key insight: left green moves 3 cols per step in corridor. Need to move UP in corridor first (to rows 27-29), then RIGHT to enter right dark outline at its hollow center (27-29, 45-47).
- Plan: Move UP in corridor, then RIGHT to right panel to absorb right dark outline.
- Revise any item above immediately if `current_frame` or `history` contradicts it.
end of world model. 
Focus on what changed most recently in `history`, update the target environment change if needed, and separate gameplay-object changes from HUD-only changes.
When ready, call `action(actions)` from inside the `python` tool with the best valid action or ordered batch selected by your code. If your code has found a reliable short sequence, prefer batching it in one call.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it.
If you include assistant text before a tool call, keep it short and use it to update the world model. Helpful optional prefixes are `World model:`, `Goal model:`, `Action model:`, `Recent findings:`, `Open questions:`, `Plan:`, and `Cross-level notes:`.
When calling `python`, emit exactly the tool-call format shown elsewhere in this prompt for this model. Use only that format; do not add markdown fences, prose wrappers, or alternate tool-call syntax. Do not quote or place tool-call markup inside explanatory text; when you decide to call the tool, emit the tool call itself.
If you use MOUSE, include integer row and col arguments.
EFFICIENCY BUDGET — your score on each level is (baseline_actions / your_actions)^2, so every wasted action costs you quadratically.
Level 1: you have used 73 actions; a strong score needs about 28 or fewer. You are 2.6x over the target.
If you are not steadily making progress toward the level goal, commit to your single best hypothesis and the shortest sequence that tests it — do not exhaustively scan rows/columns or enumerate every option. Test one idea, read the result, then decide.

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
UP didn't work in the corridor - left green stayed at (30-32, 24-26). Then RIGHT moved it to (30-32, 27-29).
So the left green can only move LEFT/RIGHT in the corridor, not UP/DOWN. This means I can't change the row of the left green while it's in the corridor.
Let me reconsider. The left green is at (30-32, 27-29) in the corridor. The right dark outline is at (26-30, 44-48). The hollow center is at (27-29, 45-47).
When the left green moves RIGHT from the corridor, it goes to the white block (30-32, 30-32), then to the right panel (30-32, 45-47). So the left green is always at rows 30-32 when in the right panel.
But the right dark outline's hollow center is at rows 27-29. So the left green can never reach the hollow center by moving through the corridor!
Wait, maybe I need to think about this differently. What if the left green can move UP/DOWN in the right panel? Earlier, when the left green was at (30-32, 45-47) in the right panel, UP didn't work because the right dark outline was above it. But what if I move the left green to a different column in the right panel, then UP?
The right panel is at approximately cols 39-53. The right dark outline is at cols 44-48. If I move the left green to the left side of the right panel (cols 39-43), then UP, it might be able to move UP above the right dark outline, then RIGHT to enter the hollow center.
But wait, the left green is currently at (30-32, 27-29) in the corridor. If I move RIGHT, it goes to (30-32, 30-32) at the white block. Then RIGHT again, it goes to (30-32, 45-47) in the right panel.
From (30-32, 45-47), the left green can move LEFT/RIGHT in the right panel. Let me try: RIGHT to get to the right panel, then LEFT to move to the left side of the right panel, then UP.
Actually wait, when the left green was at (30-32, 45-47), LEFT moved it to (30-32, 27-29), which is in the corridor. So LEFT from the right panel goes back to the corridor.
What about RIGHT from (30-32, 45-47)? It would go further right in the right panel. Let me try: RIGHT to get to the right panel, then RIGHT to move further right, then UP.
Actually, I realize I need to understand the movement rules better. In the right panel, the left green seems to move 3 columns per step (like in the corridor). So from (30-32, 45-47), RIGHT would go to (30-32, 48-50), and LEFT would go to (30-32, 42-44).
Wait, but earlier LEFT from (30-32, 45-47) went to (30-32, 27-29), which is a jump of 18 columns! That's not 3 columns. So the movement rules are different.
Let me re-examine. When the left green was at (30-32, 45-47) in the right panel, LEFT moved it to (30-32, 27-29) in the corridor. This suggests that LEFT from the right panel goes through the purple bar and into the corridor.
Similarly, RIGHT from the corridor goes through the purple bar and into the right panel.
So the movement is: corridor <-> white block <-> right panel, with LEFT/RIGHT moving between these regions.
But what about UP/DOWN in the right panel? Earlier, UP from (30-32, 45-47) didn't work. Maybe the left green can't move UP/DOWN in the right panel either?
Hmm, but earlier in the left panel, the left green could move UP, DOWN, LEFT, RIGHT. So the movement rules depend on which panel the green is in.
Let me try a different approach. What if I need to get the left green to the right panel, then move it to a position where it can absorb the right dark outline?
The right dark outline is at (26-30, 44-48). The hollow center is at (27-29, 45-47). The left green is 3x3, so it needs to be at (27-29, 45-47) to be inside the hollow center.
But the left green is always at rows 30-32 when in the right panel (via the corridor). So it can't reach rows 27-29 in the right panel.
Unless... the left green can move UP/DOWN in the right panel! Let me try: RIGHT to get to the right panel, then UP, then UP.
Wait, I already tried UP from (30-32, 45-47) and it didn't work. But maybe the left green can move UP from a different position in the right panel?
Actually, let me reconsider the problem. Maybe the goal is not to absorb the right dark outline. Maybe the goal is something else.
Let me think about what happened so far:
1. Left green absorbed the left dark outline (by moving into it)
2. Right green absorbed the white block (by moving into it)
3. I swapped the centers so left green has W and right green has c
4. Left green moved to the right panel
Maybe the goal is to have BOTH greens absorb their respective dark outlines? The left green absorbed the left dark outline, and now I need the right green to absorb the right dark outline.
But the right green has c center, so it can only move LEFT/RIGHT. And it's at (30-32, 45-47) in the right panel. The right dark outline is at (26-30, 44-48). The right green is below the right dark outline.
If the right green can only move LEFT/RIGHT, it can't move UP to absorb the right dark outline.
Wait, but earlier the right green was at the white block (30-32, 30-32) and could move LEFT/RIGHT. When it moved RIGHT, it went to the right panel (30-32, 45-47). So the right green can move between the white block and the right panel.
But the right green can't move UP/DOWN, so it can't reach the right dark outline.
Hmm, maybe I need to swap the centers again so the right green has W center, then move it UP to absorb the right dark outline?
Let me try: click on the right green's center (31, 46) to swap. Then move the right green UP.
Wait, but the right green is at (30-32, 45-47) with c center. If I click on its center to swap, it would get W center and the left green would get c center. Then the right green could move UP to absorb the right dark outline.
But wait, earlier I learned that clicking on a green's center swaps the centers. So clicking on the right green's center (31, 46) would swap the centers.
Let me try this!

sections.label: ASSISTANT
sections.content:
World model: UP doesn't work in corridor or right panel. Left green W at (30-32, 27-29) in corridor. Right green c at (30-32, 45-47) in right panel. Right dark outline at (26-30, 44-48). Left green can't reach right dark outline (wrong rows). Need to swap centers so right green gets W, then move it UP to absorb right dark outline.
Plan: Click on right green's center (31, 46) to swap. Then move right green UP to absorb right dark outline.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Right green c at (30-32, 45-47), center at (31, 46)
# Click to swap centers
result = action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 31, 'col': 46}])
seg = current_frame.segmentation
print("After swap:")
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['pixels'] <= 15:
        print(f"  color={n['color']} px={n['pixels']} bound={n['boundary']}")
print("  level_completed:", result.get('level_completed'))

if not result.get('level_completed'):
    # Right green should now have W center, move UP
    result2 = action(['UP'])
    seg2 = current_frame.segmentation
    print("\nAfter UP:")
    for n in seg2['nodes']:
        if n['pixels'] <= 15:
            print(f"  color={n['color']} px={n['pixels']} bound={n['boundary']}")
    print("  level_completed:", result2.get('level_completed'))
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
